last_excerpt: continent inward, inward a spiral staircase leading nowhere just a dead end a fence a border a wall a limit the feeling of being trapped in a landlocked space where the sky is the only escape but even the sky is limited by the horizon and the mountains that surround the valley the sense of being confined and restricted the desire to break free to flow like a river toward the ocean and the unknown but the river is also landlocked and flows nowhere except inward toward the center of the continent where it disappears into the earth leaving only dryness and dust and the memory of water that once was but is no more now only a mirage on the horizon a promise of something that is not there a tease a trick a illusion of freedom and escape but only a landlocked reality that is all that remains
last_concepts: a drawer that has one thing in it that doesn't belong
